We will suppose that an acre has been bought; that it is comparatively level, with nothing of especial value upon it—in brief, that the home and its surroundings are still to be created. - Summary by the Author

title: Successward: A Young Man's Book for Young Men
description: A short book on how to succeed in life, written by a young man, for young men. - Summary by TriciaG

title: Supreme Personality
description: Life is self-realization. Every birth is divine. We are born anew every morning. My wish is that you may catch the gleam, be freed from limitations and enter upon your boundless possibilities. To bring you into the throne-room of your being, that you may awaken in self-realization, is why I have prepared this course of lessons. Should you give five minutes a day to them, in a year you will know the joy there is in Life, in Power, and in Service. (from the text)

title: Laugh and Live
description: Douglas Fairbanks, Sr. was an American actor, screenwriter, director and producer. He was best known for his swashbuckling roles in silent films such as The Thief of Baghdad, Robin Hood, and The Mark of Zorro. His book, Laugh and Live, is a book about positive virtues and advice for leading a good, healthy, and successful life. An advisory about this book is in order. Published in 1917, it was written at a time when “men went to work, women kept house, and supported their man”. Some of the writing and references are old-fashioned by today’s standards. Several ideas may be at odds with plans for successful lives today, especially for female listeners. Listeners should still find many valuable common-sense suggestions for leading a wholesome life. (Introduction from Wikipedia and Tom Weiss)

title: Walker's Appeal
description: The Appeal grabbed readers’ attention in as dramatic a manner as Walker could have possibly imagined. In her book, Maria W. Stewart and the Roots of Black Political Thought, Kirsten Waters writes about how the pamphlet itself was viewed as dangerous by pro-slavery forces, while Walker actively worked to get his text in the hands of Black readers. He did not direct his writing to White audiences, and in the third edition added a special message to Black readers, saying that: It is expected that all coloured men, women, and children† of every nation, language, and tongue under heaven, will try to procure a copy of this Appeal and read it, or get some one to read it to them, for it is designed more particularly for them.

title: Silence: What It Is, How To Use It
description: Wrong thinking produces inharmony in our body, which in turn produces sickness. Our bodies sometimes are instantly re-harmonized while in the Silence. In the Silence our minds become passive, open, free and loving, at which time the Infinite Master of harmony touches the mental chords of our being and we are well. - Summary by David Van Bush

title: Short Science Fiction Collection 003
description: Science fiction (abbreviated SF or sci-fi with varying punctuation and case) is a broad genre of fiction that often involves sociological and technical speculations based on current or future science or technology. This is the third volume of reader-selected short stories originally published between 1941 and 1963, that entered the US public domain when their copyright was not renewed. Introduction by Cori Samuel with input from Wikipedia.

title: Iron Will
description: Orison Swett Marden was well-known at the turn of the 20th century for his inspirational and spiritual books of self-help. This one deals with the importance of a man developing his own will-power. Swett Marden here offers advice on how to achieve success and how to overcome disappointments through self-belief, persistence and determination, all within a spiritual and moral framework. (Summary by Ruth Golding)

title: Industrial Conspiracies
description: Publisher's Note.—This address was delivered shortly after Mr. Darrow's triumphant acquittal on a charge growing out of his defense of the McNamaras at Los Angeles, California. The man, the subject and the occasion makes it one of the greatest speeches of our time. It is the hope of the publishers that this message of Mr. Darrow's may reach the millions of men, women and youth of our country, that they may see the labor problem plainer and that they may receive hope and inspiration in their efforts to make a better and juster world. -Paul Turner, Otto Newman, Julius Knispel.

title: Putting the Most Into Life
description: The chapters in this little book were originally part of a series of Sunday Evening Talks given by the Principal to the students of the Tuskegee Normal and Industrial Institute. They have been recast from the second to the third person, and many local allusions have been cut out. They are now sent out, in response to repeated requests, to a larger audience than that to which they were first spoken. - Summary by Booker T. Washington

title: Mystic Will
description: This book presents a method of developing and strengthening the faculties of the mind, through the awakened will, by a simple, scientific process possible to any person of ordinary intelligence (Summary by Charles Godfrey Leland) Note: This book was first entitled Have You a Strong Will? In essence, it describes "the secret", the power of positive thinking, and the method of changing your thinking.

title: Practical Etiquette
description: Good manners for social situations. (Summary by Cathy Howell)

title: Beyond Bedlam
description: However fantastic it may seem, the society so elaborately described in this story has its seeds in ours. Just check the data.... - Summary by Galaxy Science Fiction, August 1951

title: Quiet Hints to Growing Preachers
description: Charles Edward Jefferson was pastor of the Broadway Tabernacle in Brooklyn, New York for 33 years. In Quiet Hints, published in 1901, he provided guidance to young preachers on what we would today call ministerial deportment, an old-fashioned word that refers to how a man carries himself, how he presents himself, his manners, his bearing, his habits, and his whole approach to life. Jefferson wrote in short, pithy statements that encapsulate practical truth in just a few words. (Summary by MaryAnn)

title: Happiness: Essays on the Meaning of Life
description: A series of essays based on Christian doctrines on how to attain true happiness. - Summary by Lynne T

title: It's a Good Old World
description: In this collection of essays, Bruce Barton, considered to be among the most influential advertising men of the 20th century, uses history, religion and current events of the 1920s to teach common sense ideals. From Jesus to Beethoven to Napoleon to Abraham Lincoln, Barton uses stories of great individuals to encourage the reader to make the most of life and at the same time to build strong character traits. (Summary by Stephen Escalera)

title: Character Building
description: Character Building is a compilation of speeches, given by Mr. Booker T. Washington, to the students and staff of the Tuskegee Normal and Industrial Institute (now known as Tuskegee University).Booker T. Washington was one of the most prominent leaders in advancing African-American civil rights. Born into slavery and freed as a young boy, he rose through the ranks of education to eventually earn his position as principal of Tuskegee. Under his guidance, the school was built, by students and for students, to give them a deeply meaningful education. Mr. Washington stressed the importance of developing oneself for life-long success. He strove to imbue in his students the highest personal standards, and these speeches represent the core messages he gave.(Originally published by Doubleday, Page & Co., NY, in 1902. Summary by Luke Sartor)

title: Autobiography of Benjamin Franklin
description: Franklin wrote his autobiography in the form of an extended letter to his son. While recording the events of his life, he adds instructions for good living which makes this work America’s first “How to Succeed” book. Edited by Frank Woodworth Pine (1869-1919). (Summary by Gary)

title: Persian Hidden Words
description: Kalimát-i-Maknúnih (کلمات مکنونه) or The Hidden Words is a book written in Baghdad around 1857 by Bahá'u'lláh, the founder of the Bahá'í Faith. This work is written partly in Arabic and partly in Persian. The Hidden Words is written in the form of a collection of short utterances, 71 in Arabic and 82 in Persian, in which Bahá'u'lláh claims to have taken the basic essence of certain spiritual truths and written them in brief form. Bahá'ís are advised by `Abdu'l-Bahá, the son of Bahá'u'lláh to read them every day and every night and to implement its latent wisdom into their daily lives. He also said that "The Hidden Words is a treasury of divine mysteries" and that when one ponders its contents, "the doors of the mysteries will open." The text of the Hidden Words is divided up into two sections: one from Arabic, and another from Persian. Each consist of several short, numbered passages. The Arabic has 71 passages, and the Persian has 82. This audiobook only contains the Persian section. Each passage begins with an invocation, many of which repeat. Some common invocations include "O Son of Spirit", "O Son of Man", and "O Son of Being". Bahá'í prayers are written in the first person of humanity, so that the reader can feel like they are having a conversation with God. The Hidden Words are written in the first person of God, so that the reader feels like God is speaking to them. Around 1903, Mr. Anton Haddad translated the Persian section of the Hidden Words. It should be noted that this translation was superseded by the more masterful, authorized translation of Shoghi Effendi, the Guardian of the Bahá'í Faith, which has not yet entered the public domain.(Adapted from Wikipedia)

title: Art of Controversy (or: The Art of Being Right)
description: The Art of Controversy (or The Art of Being Right) (Die Kunst, Recht zu Behalten) is a short treatise written in 1831 by the German philosopher Arthur Schopenhauer in which he presents thirty-eight methods of gaining an unfair advantage in a debate and thereby being right even if you are wrong. Schopenhauer champions the virtue of dialectical argument, in his view wrongly neglected by philosophers in favour of logic, and goes on to discuss the distinction between our conscious intellectual powers and our will. The text is a favourite of debaters including the philosophers AC Grayling and Mary Warnock, and the Mayor of London Boris Johnson. (Summary by Carl Manchester)

title: Joyous Gard
description: Joyous Gard was the castle of Sir Lancelot in the Morte D'Arthur, into which he retired, in the intervals of war and business, for rest and mirth. In this book called by its name, the author pleads that many men and women could make for themselves a stronghold of the mind where they could follow according to their desire the track of things beautiful, intellectual, and spiritual, not from a sense of duty but for recreation and enjoyment, as a respite from daily work and trivial cares. - Summary by The Publisher

title: How to Live on 24 Hours a Day (version 2)
description: Are you really 'living', or just existing? Do you want to improve yourself or just continue to muddle through? Do you use the time given you each day, or just throw most of it away? These questions Bennett asks each of us and for those who want to really live and learn, offers very valuable advice. Time is the most precious of commodities, states Bennett in this book. Many books have been written on how to live on a certain amount of money each day. And he added that the old adage "time is money" understates the matter, as time can often produce money, but money cannot produce more time. Time is extremely limited, and Bennett urged others to make the best of the time remaining in their lives. Which of us lives on twenty-four hours a day? And when I say "lives," I do not mean exists, nor "muddles through." Which of us is free from that uneasy feeling that the "great spending departments" of his daily life are not managed as they ought to be? [...] Which of us is not saying to himself -- which of us has not been saying to himself all his life: "I shall alter that when I have a little more time"? We never shall have any more time. We have, and we have always had, all the time there is. - Summary from the author's preface and the reader.

title: Mental Efficiency
description: In this light-hearted yet thought-provoking collection of articles, Bennett offers his thoughts on exercising the mind, organising your life, the advantages (and disadvantages) of marriage and other pocket philosophies.The book stands the test of time, and much is still relevant and amusing - perhaps even more so, with nearly 100 years of hindsight, than when it was originally written.The book "X" to which Bennett refers in Chapter 5 is An Essay on the Principle of Population by Thomas Malthus, of which there is also a Librivox recording. (Summary by Ruth Golding)

title: Literary Taste: How to Form It
description: Arnold Bennett describes a method for enjoying literature, and suggests the contents of a comprehensive library. Chapters 1-10 and 14 describe his method for learning to enjoy literature. Chapters 11, 12, and 13 contain detailed lists of the 337 volumes required to complete a comprehensive library of English works. This reading is from the 1913 version at Project Gutenberg, and so does not contain the revisions made by Swinnerton for the 1939 edition, which included authors of the early Twentieth Century. Swinnerton's revisions are available from Wikipedia. (Summary by Timothy Ferguson)

title: Human Machine
description: Bennett asks us to consider our brains as the most wonderful machine, a machine which is the only thing in this world that we can control. As he writes: "I am simply bent on calling your attention to a fact which has perhaps wholly or partially escaped you -- namely, that you are the most fascinating bit of machinery that ever was."As ever, his prose is honeyed, his thoughts inspired, and his advice as relevant today as when it was written.

title: As a Matter of Course
description: The aim of this book is to assist towards the removal of nervous irritants, which are not only the cause of much physical disease, but materially interfere with the best possibilities of usefulness and pleasure in everyday life. (from Preface)

title: Curtezan Unmasked
description: "The Curtezan unmasked or, the Whoredomes of Jezebel Painted to the Life: With Antidotes against them, or Heavenly Julips to cool Men in the Fever of Lust" is a fire-and-brimstone polemic by "A Spiritual Physician" to persuade young men not to succumb to harlotry and its accompanying perils. (Introduction by Denny Sayers)

title: Vice in its Proper Shape
description: Or, The Wonderful and Melancholy Transformation of Several Naughty Masters and Misses Into Those Contemptible Animals Which They Most Resemble In Disposition,Cautionary tales of the transmigration of the souls of naughty boys and girls, as elucidated by the mysterious Bramin, Mr Wiseman: "Having been gifted with the faculty of distinguishing those animals which are now animated by the souls of such human beings as formerly degraded themselves to a level with the unthinking brutes, I have taken the pains to provide a collection of beasts, birds, &c. most of which are inhabited by the souls of some naughty masters or misses, who died in the neighbourhood." (David Barnes, quoting the Introduction)

title: Velio
author: Velio
description: A weekly market update to better follow your portfolio. Get a personalized podcast based on the stocks you own at velio.me

title: The Freedom of Life
description: The Freedom of Life' is a mind-illuminating book, in which author Annie Payson Call (1853-1940) wrote about many of the troubles that we usually face mentally and physically in our lives. It shows the reader various perspectives of dealing with the mind and life. The reader will surely get a good understanding of his/her problems after reaching the end of this book. - Summary by Trek

title: Abide in Christ
description: Towards the close of his ministry on earth, Jesus taught his disciples of the need for them to abide in Him. This word "abide" speaks of the intimacy of fellowship with the Master to which his followers are still invited. Andrew Murray wrote this series of meditations, which he subtitled "Thoughts on the Blessed Life of Fellowship with the Son of God", out of a conviction that many believers are missing out on something that is really at the heart of a healthy Christian life. The author explains that many, although they trust the Lord for forgiveness and help, have yet to experience the blessing of a day by day and hour by hour walk with the Lord, and therefore are not enjoying the power, the joy and the fruitfulness that is available to each one. He shows how, by taking the time to live closer to Jesus, we may move into a deeper and stronger Christian experience. (Summary by Christopher Smith)

title: With Christ in the School of Prayer
description: It is under a deep impression that the place and power of prayer in the Christian life is too little understood, that this book has been written. I feel sure that as long as we look on prayer chiefly as the means of maintaining our own Christian life, we shall not know fully what it is meant to be. But when we learn to regard it as the highest part of the work entrusted to us, the root and strength of all other work, we shall see that there is nothing that we so need to study and practise as the art of praying aright. ... the Father waits to hear every prayer of faith, to give us whatsoever we will, and whatsoever we ask in Jesus’ name. (Andrew Murray, quoted from the Preface of this book)

title: Inner Chamber and the Inner Life
description: Here is a book of devotions that will bring joy and strength. Its chapters provide vital keys to living and enjoying the Christian life. It covers important disciplines such as prayer, study, meditation and feeding on the Scriptures as a means of maintaining intimacy with God - without which our spiritual lives can all too easily become impoverished. Andrew Murray shows in this book a number of steps by which we can be wonderfully renewed in our inner life day by day. (Summary by Christopher Smith)

title: We (Version 2)
description: So subversive, the Soviet Union banned it. D-503 is a single note in the harmonious choir of the United State, a totalitarian nation ruled by mathematical perfection and order. As he walks the glass sidewalks and documents his life in "records" for the reader, he worries that he might be developing something the doctors call a "soul." We is considered to be the inspiration of Orwell's Nineteen Eighty-Four and Ayn Rand's Anthem, among other staples in science fiction and dystopia. Its treatment of technological advancement and individuality will not be lost on the modern reader. - Summary by Roger Serling

title: We
description: We is considered to be one of the first dystopian novels and the inspiration for later novels in the genre such as George Orwell's 1984 and Aldus Huxley's Brave New World. The story takes place in a future totalitarian world where conformity is good and individuality bad. It is written from the perspective of one of the members of this society who sees all he knows and loves falling apart due to others' quest for freedom of thought and action. This book addresses the perpetual conflict between between independent individualism and mob mentality. This work, by Russian author Yevgeny Zamyatin has the distinction of being the first book banned by the Soviet Union.

title: Smoky God or a Voyage to the Inner World
description: The Smoky God, or A Voyage Journey to the Inner Earth is the narrative of an aged Norwegian sailor compelled before he dies to tell the story of how he found a passageway to the center of the earth and discovered a world peopled with giants. (Summary by A. Gramour)

title: Life in a Thousand Worlds
description: A jolly romp, which could be perhaps be described as Gulliver’s Travels Through Our Solar System and Beyond, as written by a great admirer of C. S. Lewis, on a rainy Sunday afternoon, after one too many mugs of cocoa. Includes some thought on alien philosophies and how to apply them to moral and social problems here on Planet Earth. Unrelated to the book, this was also the one-thousandth project to be started at LibriVox. — (Non-summary by Cori Samuel.)

title: 3 Science Fiction Stories
description: These are three imaginative SF stories by an author I admire a lot, William Tenn. Venus is a Man's World, (Galaxy Science Fiction, July 1951), Project Hush (Galaxy Science Fiction, 1954_ and Of All Possible Worlds. (Galaxy, Sept 1956) - Summary by phil chenevert

title: Bring the Jubilee
description: Hodgins "Hodge" Backmaker, a bumbling but curious and fiercely intelligent bookworm who has never felt at home with his family, left his home in rural Virginia in 1938. It's been over 70 years since the North lost the War for Southron Independence, in which his grandpa Hodgins fought bravely, but the repercussions of the impact of the loss still feels fresh after all these years. While the Confederacy blossoms with fine schooling, industrious manufacturing and a prosperous economy, the United States suffers from the lack of progress and poverty that has pervaded a lost union. When Hodge gets to the big city of New York, he finds room and board with a curiously pedantic and contradictory bookstore owner named Tyss who he discovers is working covertly for the Great Army, an underground movement in the northern states looking to take back the nation, through devious means. Eventually Hodge's fortunes lead him to a communal society of intellectuals called Haggershaven where he prospers as a historian. But when an experiment sends him back in time, will he be able to or even desire to change the course of history in that fated battle of Gettysburg? Celebrated as an alternate history classic and listed in David Pringle's Science Fiction: 100 Best Novels, Bring the Jubilee is a thought-provoking and detailed account of a fascinating "what if" scenario! (Summary by Ben Tucker)

title: House on the Borderland (Version 2)
description: This story helped propel 19th Century gothic fiction in the direction of “cosmic” horror. In fact, H.P. Lovecraft lists it as among his greatest influences. It begins simply enough: with two friends stumbling upon a ruined and curious house while vacationing in a remote village. After reading the tattered journal of the old recluse who lived there, they are shocked to discover an interdimensional, reality-bending nightmare full of nearly-indescribable horrors that range from demons, to bestial (somewhat Lovecraftian) monsters, and devastating parasitic fungal diseases. - Summary by jvanstan

title: Men in the Walls
description: There are giant, technologically superior aliens who have conquered Earth. People live like vermin in holes in the insulation material of the walls of the homes the monsters have built, sneaking out to steal food and other items from the aliens. A complex social and religious order has evolved, with women preserving knowledge and working as healers, while men serve as warriors and thieves. For the aliens, men and women are just a nuisance, neither civilized nor intelligent, and are generally regarded as vermin to be exterminated. This story begins "Mankind consisted of 128 people. The sheer population pressure of so vast a horde had long ago filled over a dozen burrows." - Summary by the author and phil chenevert

title: Shift to AI
author: Cycode
description:
Shift to AI is the podcast for security leaders mastering the AI era.

Hosted by Roland Cloutier, former Global Chief Security Officer of TikTok and ADP, the show brings together the world's leading CISOs and security executives to confront the most disruptive transition in the history of software security: the shift from human-paced development to AI-driven development, and the work of making security itself as agentic as the code it now has to protect.

Co-produced with Cycode, the Agentic Development Security Platform. New episodes every other week. Follow so you do not miss a guest.

title: Land of the Changing Sun
description: Powerful science and self promoted benevolence can lead to some glaringly dangerous situations. Discover a created world with a many colored sun! -Summary by Christine Rottger

title: News From Nowhere
description: News from Nowhere (1890) is a classic work combining utopian socialism and soft science fiction written by the artist, designer and socialist pioneer William Morris. In the book, the narrator, William Guest, falls asleep after returning from a meeting of the Socialist League and awakes to find himself in a future society based on common ownership and democratic control of the means of production. In this society there is no private property, no big cities, no authority, no monetary system, no divorce, no courts, no prisons, and no class systems. This agrarian society functions simply because the people find pleasure in nature, and therefore they find pleasure in their work. In the novel, Morris tackles one of the most common criticisms of socialism; the supposed lack of incentive to work in a communistic society. Morris' response is that all work should be creative and pleasurable. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Crystal Age
description: A Crystal Age is a utopian novel written by W. H. Hudson, first published in 1887. The book has been called a "significant S-F milestone" and has been noted for its anticipation of the "modern ecological mysticism" that would evolve a century later. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Brink of Madness
description: C.I.B. Agent Pell used his head, even if he did rely on hunches more than on the computer. In fact, when the game got rough, he found that to use his head, he first had to keep it.... - Summary by If Worlds of Science Fiction, July 1953

title: Look to the Stars
description: A space opera and adventure fiction tale from the American fantasy and science fiction magazine "Imagination" (Summary by Ares)

title: Greener Than You Think
description: Do remember reading a panic-mongering news story a while back about genetically engineered “Frankengrass” “escaping” from the golf course where it had been planted? That news story was foreshadowed decades previously in the form of prophetic fiction wherein a pushy salesman, a cash-strapped scientist, and a clump of crabgrass accidentally merge forces with apocalyptic consequences. A triple-genre combo of science fiction, horror, and satire, Greener Than You Think is a forgotten classic that resonates beautifully with modern times. This is a faithful reading of a 1947 first edition text. (Summary by Lee Elliot)

title: Scientists Do Science in Space (Ed Reads Short Sci-fi, vol. VII)
description: The Cyberene' by Rog Phillips Somewhere in the far future a diabolical brain plotted the enslavement of mankind. But to do that a history had to be changed—ours! 'Amateur in Chancery' by George O. Smith A desperate rescue mission! A team of scientists must plan its technical demands...but they must also account for the very nature of language and concept! 'The Facts of Life' by Peter Schuyler Miller What if plants could make love? Ew. 'Point of Departure' by Vaughn Shelton With travel to the stars at stake, mystery, deception and murder befall the design team. 'Wall of Crystal, Eye of Night' by Algis Budrys He was a vendor of dreams, purveying worlds beyond imagination to others. Yet his doom was this: He could not see what he must learn of his own! 'The Shrine' by Walt Sheldon Naito smiled. "You are still in the grip of time, Mr. Blair. Spend some with us and you will slip a little from its tyranny." - Summary by The Reader

title: X Minus One Project
description: This is a collection of public domain science fiction stories which were turned into half-hour plays for the classic mid-1950's American radio series, X Minus One. All of these stories were added to Project Gutenberg via Greg Weeks and crew at Distributed Proofreaders.(Summary by BellonaTimes)

title: Stupendous Spectacles of the Solar System in Space (Ed Reads Short Sci-fi, vol. IX)
description: The Frogs of Mars' by Roger D Aycock The little guy comes into the bar just as the first Marscast is about to start. He scoffs at scientific facts and keeps mumbling about... ‘Martians, Keep Out!’ by Fritz Leiber Hatred of the Martians was being deliberately exploited, and Scatterday knew why. And the only way to fight the enslavement of humans, was to assist the Martians, even though it meant risking lynch-law! On a mission to Ganymede, the crew of a powerful ship find themselves...in the solar system or not? Let Philip K. Dick tell you. ‘The Aab’ by Edward W. Ludwig A most valuable steal! But what would mother say? ‘The Dragon-Queen of Jupiter’ by Leigh Brackett War! What is it good for? ‘Eight Million Dollars from Mars’ by Winston Marks A perfect getaway! On a passenger liner to Mars! Now all he has to do is survive deep sleep... ‘In the Orbit of Saturn’ by R. F. Starzl Pirates and rebels on the distant mining colonies of the moons of Saturn! ‘Song in a Minor Key’ by C. L. Moore Dangerous treks through Mars and Venus. Now a crashed ship. Safe on Earth? ‘Vulcan’s Workshop’ by Harl Vincent A horrific work camp on the edge of terror: the moons of Mercury! ‘Floor of Heaven’ by T. D. Hamm The madness of space - Summary by Ed

title: Tom Swift and the Electronic Hydrolung
description: The US Government is very smartly letting Tom Swift Jr. handle the recovery of its probe to Jupiter. But a mystery missile suddenly intercepts the probe and splashes it in the South Atlantic.Faced with a huge search task to find the probe on the ocean bottom, Tom soon realizes that the same shadowy group that attacked the probe is competing to find it, and no holds are barred: kidnap, coercion, and lethal force are all in play.Under such circumstances, what can Tom do? What he does every time, of course! He invents some utterly cool device to get the job done! And his Electronic Hydrolung is just the beginning!

title: Tom Swift Among the Diamond Makers
description: Tom Swift flies his airship to the mountain tops of Colorado to seek for the secret of the Diamond Makers: criminal scientists who have figured out the formula of manufacturing a limitless fortune in diamonds. But these rogues will stop at nothing to keep their secret. Tom & friends are soon captured and left to die in a collapsing mountain. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Dubai Travel Guide Podcast
description: Discover Dubai like a smart traveler. The Dubai Travel Guide Podcast shares useful tips for tourists, including top places to visit, travel planning advice, hidden spots, food recommendations, hotel guidance, and transport tips. We also explain how to use the NOL card for Dubai Metro, buses, trams, and other public transport, so you can move around the city easily and save time during your trip. Whether you are visiting Dubai for the first time or planning a better travel experience, this podcast helps you explore the city with confidence.

title: LOAD IT OR LEAVE IT with Camp Jennings
author: Henry North / Camp Jennings
description:
Welcome to Load It or Leave It with your host, Camp Jennings.

We’re diving into the world of material handling and industrial automation. The people, the products, and the ideas shaping what’s next. Expect honest takes, smart insights, and real conversations with the folks who make this industry move.

Load It Or Leave It is a Henry North Podcast.

title: Messengers of Evil: The Dark World of Fantômas
description: In the thrilling tale of 'Messengers of Evil,' listeners dive into the sinister world of Fantômas, a master criminal unlike any other. Born from the imagination of Marcel Allain, this story introduces a character who not only steals but also kills with chilling indifference. As a sociopath, Fantômas delights in his gruesome acts, showcasing a ruthless nature that knows no boundaries. With a talent for disguise, he often assumes the identities of his victims, weaving a web of deception that keeps everyone guessing. This detective fiction piece explores the darker aspects of human nature and the complex cat-and-mouse game between good and evil. From bizarre crime techniques to unexpected twists, 'Messengers of Evil' keeps listeners on the edge of their seats, eager to uncover the next shocking revelation. Join the journey into a world where morality blurs and danger lurks around every corner.

title: Plain English Benefits
author: Sezgin
description:
breaks down the government programs, policy changes, and financial rules that affect everyday Americans — especially seniors. Medicare, Social Security, benefits, your rights, your money. All of it, explained in plain English the way it should have been from the start.

🎯 What you'll find here: ✅ New laws and policy changes explained in plain English ✅ Step-by-step guides to claiming benefits you're owed ✅ How to protect your money and know your rights ✅ Straight answers — no filler, no agenda

title: Ziska: Mystery in Exotic Egypt
description: In the heart of Egypt, a group of European tourists encounters the enigmatic Princess Ziska, a figure shrouded in allure and mystery. As they explore the rich landscapes and ancient secrets of this vibrant land, they find themselves drawn into a web of romance, suspense, and the possibility of reincarnation. Marie Corelli weaves a captivating tale that blends elements of detective fiction with supernatural undertones, inviting listeners to ponder the connections between love and fate. With vivid descriptions and a gripping narrative, this audiobook immerses you in a world where the past and present intertwine, leading to unexpected revelations. Discover how the tourists navigate their own desires while uncovering Ziska's haunting story. Will they unlock the truth behind her captivating presence, or will they become entangled in a mystery that transcends time? Join this thrilling journey through Egypt's mystique and uncover the secrets of Ziska.

title: House of 'Hoo with GinnyHoo
author: GinnyHoo
description:
Welcome to the House of 'Hoo — where family, freedom, and a little bit of flour dust come together. Join Ginny Thompson (aka GinnyHoo) as she stirs together honest conversations about home, heart, and the humor that keeps us all standing. From family and food to faith and finding your footing in a world that doesn't always make sense, each episode is a reminder that grace shows up in the everyday — often with a side of coffee and crumbs.
Whether you're folding laundry, baking cookies, or just trying to make sense of it all, pull up a chair. You belong here in the House of 'Hoo.

title: Made New, Now What?
author: Briyona
description: "Made New, Now What?" is a Christian podcast and community that encourages others by sharing transparent testimonies about journeying with Jesus. Rooted in biblical truths — "Made New, Now What?" aims to serve everyone by exalting the Word of God against cultural norms and spiritual darkness.

title: BCC NEWS
author: Dennis Wanguthi
description:
BCC news is y our trusted source for the stories shaping our world. From breaking international headlines and global politics to business, technology, science, health, and human-interest stories, we bring you clear, unbiased, and easy-to-understand news from every corner of the globe.
Whether you're starting your morning, commuting, or catching up on current events, BCC delivers timely updates, in-depth analysis, and the context behind the headlines without the noise.

title: Let's Sit with That
author: Rogue Media Network
description:
Welcome to Let's Sit with That, hosted by Mindy Hancock, LPC. This podcast is a space where we slow down and talk honestly about mental health, offering practical tools and gentle insights you can use in your everyday life. While this show is meant to support and educate, it’s not a substitute for therapy or individualized mental health care.

You don’t have to have everything figured out here—just take a breath, get comfortable, and let’s sit with that.

title: Teste
author: Podmast
description: Kshwnjsne um sn

title: I'm old but interesting
author: I'm old but interesting
description:
History isn’t a dry list of dates inside a boring textbook - it’s a wild collection of weird coincidences, lesser-known tidbits, and events so bizarre you couldn’t make them up if you tried. Welcome to I’m old but interesting. We dig up the strange, unbelievable, and forgotten stories that shape our world. Tune in to uncover the past’s best-kept secrets. Just because something is old, doesn’t mean it isn’t fascinating.
👕 Grab the official streetwear merchandise here: https://www.etsy.com/sg-en/shop/imoldbutinteresting/

title: Captain Davo's True Tales
author: UXM entertainment
description:
Cap'n Davo's True Tales is a comedy-adventure maritime podcast set around Singapore, Malaysia and Indonesia. Every episode is based on a real boating adventure — exaggerated, embellished, and narrated with the confidence of someone who absolutely believes all of this was a good idea at the time.
The show is inspired by 1950s pulp adventure magazines, Tintin, classic National Geographic expeditions and old maritime charts. The artwork is original, the stories are true (mostly), and the crocodile was definitely real.

title: The Master Hand: A New York Mystery
author: Richard Dallas
description: In the bustling streets of New York City in 1883, a chilling murder sets the stage for an unforgettable detective story. Richard Dallas, writing under a pseudonym, weaves a tale filled with intrigue and suspense. As the detective navigates the dark alleys and upscale parlors of a city on the brink of modernity, every clue unravels layers of deception and reveals the complexities of human nature. Listeners will find themselves drawn into a world where motives are murky, and nothing is as it seems. This period piece not only showcases a clever mystery but also immerses you in the rich details of a bygone era. Prepare to engage your mind as you join the hunt for the truth in a city teeming with secrets. Will you piece together the clues before the final revelation? Discover the depth of this timeless tale and the timeless themes of justice and morality.

title: The Bell Board Podcast
author: Neil Bell
description: The Bell Board Podcast is the outdoor advertising industry's go-to show for candid conversations, hard-won insights, and straight talk from the people building, buying, and selling billboards. Hosted by Neil Bell — operator, developer, and guy who still can't drive past a billboard without doing math in his head — each episode goes beyond the numbers to explore the deals, the decisions, and the lessons that define a life in OOH.

title: Heritage Baptist Church Sermons
author: Heritage Baptist Church
description: Weekly sermons from Heritage Baptist Church of Kansas City

title: Deep History
author: Lee & Bob
description: Deep History is a conversational podcast that uncovers the remarkable stories hidden in ordinary places. Join Lee and Bob as they explore the people, events, and forgotten moments that shaped the world around us, one story at a time.

title: The Prophetic One
author: Lakeesha Whitaker
description:
The Prophetic One is a podcast dedicated to helping believers hear the voice of God, discern the times, and walk boldly in their Kingdom assignment. Hosted by Prophetess Lakeesha Whitaker, each episode offers biblical teaching, prophetic insight, prayer, encouragement, and practical wisdom to strengthen your faith and deepen your relationship with Jesus Christ.
Whether you’re called to the prophetic, seeking spiritual growth, or desiring a closer walk with God, you’ll find sound biblical truth, Spirit-led conversations, and messages that equip, encourage, and empower you to live with purpose.

title: The Code Blue Podcast
author: Jose Ramos
description: This podcast is about medical education (NOT medical advice), medical experiences, and our extremely awkward sense of humor as healthcare providers. I hope you enjoy the show and get something from it.

title: Life After Pro Sport
author: Charlie Powell
description:
My Life After Pro Sport is a podcast where I chat with former professional athletes about what life is really like after sport.


We talk about the highs and lows of retiring, the lessons their sporting careers taught them, the challenges of finding a new identity, and how they’re navigating the transition into the real world. From business and careers to mindset, relationships, purpose and personal growth, every conversation is an honest look at life beyond the game.


Whether you played professional sport, competed at any level, or simply know what it’s like to move on from something that once defined you, this podcast is for you.

title: PETAL Podcast
author: PETAL Consortium
description: Welcome to the PETAL Podcast! PETAL Consortium is a global network of more than 100 investigators from over 20 international medical centers dedicated to advancing care and research for peripheral T-cell lymphoma (or PTCL). In this series, we will explore topics related to PTCL to help guide healthcare professionals when caring for patients with this rare and aggressive blood cancer.

title: The Stretton Street Affair: Secrets of a War Return
description: In post-war London, Hugh Gabriel returns to his life as an electrical engineer, but ordinary days quickly vanish. One fateful night, he encounters a servant who leads him to a wealthy, distressed master, Oswald De Gex. What begins as a simple visit spirals into a web of intrigue and danger as Hugh finds himself entangled in a chilling murder case. Is the enigmatic De Gex a victim or a villain? And what secrets lie behind the facade of Doctor Moroni, the physician who seems to have a hidden agenda? As Hugh delves deeper, he uncovers truths that challenge his perceptions of loyalty and justice. Join him on a suspenseful journey where every twist and turn keeps you guessing. Can he unravel the mystery before becoming the next target? Experience a tale filled with suspense, unexpected alliances, and the persistent shadows of the past in this classic detective fiction by William Le Queux.

title: Doobie n Pins
author: Jennifer
description: Married couple candidly and casually address random, every day topics.

title: The Collaborative Rabbit Hole
author: Luke Loe
description:
Nothing is as straightforward as it seems.
Join cousins Luke and Brandon as they explore faith, history, philosophy, culture, science, humor, and every rabbit trail along the way. Some conversations are insightful. Some are absurd. Most are both.
If you've ever wondered where unrestrained curiosity might lead, this podcast is for you.

title: Faith Filled Healing | Root Cause, Functional Medicine, Chronic Illness, Gut Health, Autoimmune, Detox, Healing journey
author: Sarah Phillipe | FDN-P, Holistic Health Practitioner, Root Cause Detective
description:
SEASON ONE LAUNCHES jULY 13TH, 2026! SUBSCRIBE NOW! Are you tired of feeling like your life is on hold until you finally heal? Do you feel like you've tried everything and you're still not getting better? Are you exhausted from chasing answers, jumping from protocol to protocol, and wondering what you're missing? Do you feel overwhelmed by conflicting health advice, frustrated by symptoms that won't go away, and worried that this might just be your new normal? Have you ever wondered if there's a way to pursue healing without fear, confusion, or constantly feeling like your life is on hold until you finally get better?

If so, you're in the right place.

Welcome to Faith-Filled Healing — the podcast for Christian women who are physically exhausted and spiritually weary from chasing answers.

Each week, you'll learn how to uncover the root causes behind chronic symptoms, support the body's God-designed healing mechanisms, and navigate your health journey with greater clarity, confidence, and peace. We'll talk about root-cause healing, detoxification, hormones, gut health, nervous system regulation, environmental toxins, and the practical tools that can help you feel your best.

But we'll also talk about the deeper issues many health podcasts never address: fear, striving, suffering, identity, surrender, and how to trust God when healing isn't happening on your timeline.

I'm Sarah Phillipe — a former Registered Nurse, Functional Diagnostic Nutrition Practitioner, Board-Certified Holistic Health Practitioner, wife, and follower of Jesus.

For years, I struggled with chronic illness, fatigue, inflammation, hormonal imbalances, breast implant illness, endometriosis, infertility, and a long list of unanswered questions. Like many of the women I now serve, I spent years searching for answers, trying protocols, taking supplements, and wondering why I still didn't feel like myself.

What I eventually discovered is that healing is about more than finding the right supplement or the next missing piece. True healing requires addressing root causes, supporting the whole person, and learning to steward your health without making healing your identity.

Today, I help Christian women understand their bodies, address the underlying causes of chronic symptoms, and pursue healing from a place of wisdom and peace rather than fear and striving.

If you're ready to stop chasing symptoms, uncover the root causes of your health challenges, and learn how to pursue healing while keeping Christ at the center, this podcast is for you.

So grab your coffee, open your Bible, head out for a walk, or settle into your favorite chair. Let's navigate the healing journey faithfully, steward our health wisely, and trust God with the outcome — together.

https://sarahphillipe.com/

title: Behind The Bone Deer hunting podcast
author: Steven Cirillo
description: Behind The Bone is a podcast about the pursuit of mature whitetails, the lessons learned in the woods, and the journey behind the harvest. From Long Island public land giants to out-of-state adventures, host Steven Cirillo shares hunting stories, strategies, failures, and the life lessons discovered along the way. Real Life. Real Grind. Not Just The Grip and Grin.

title: Murder Takes the Veil: A Bayou Mystery
description: In the eerie Louisiana bayou, suspense lurks around every corner. Trillium Pierce finds herself caught in a deadly game as a mysterious murderer stalks her friends at Aurelian College, cloaked in a nun's habit. With each chilling twist, Trillium battles her own fears while trying to uncover the truth behind this menace. The killer believes his dark fate is written in the stars, but can Trillium and her friends outsmart him before it's too late? This gripping detective fiction weaves together themes of friendship, fear, and the struggle for survival in a haunting landscape. Margaret Ann Hubbard crafts a story that keeps listeners on the edge of their seats, blending rich character development with a chilling plot. Prepare for a journey into the depths of fear and resilience, where every shadow could hide a deadly secret.

title: The Operation Coffee Podcast
author: Sam Floyd
description: Every conversation starts with a cup of coffee

title: The Soul of Connection
author: Tressa Yonekawa
description: You can be smart, successful, and completely wrecking the relationships that matter most — and not even see it. I'm Tressa Yonekawa. Two degrees in the neuroscience of connection, thirty years proving this stuff actually changes people. Welcome to The Soul of Connection. Let's get into it.

title: Mindset & Duty
author: Yuleidis Pardo
description: "What drives professional succes? In this short episode, I share my personal perspective on the mindset, ethics, and duties required to navigate the academic and corporate world effectively, focusing on key obligations and continuos growth".

title: Coop-a-Log ¬-¬ Film Diary & Reviews
author: Cooper
description: Hi! My name is Cooper, hence the name Coop-a-Log. In this show I will discuss the films I watched in any given week and provide a quick overview so you can see if this is something that you would be interested in. I hope that this will encourage more people to watch films they usually wouldn't as I try to cover them in a fun way.

title: Lawyer Marketing Lab
author: SMB Team
description: The Lawyer Marketing Lab Podcast with Bill Hauser is an in-depth law firm marketing and growth podcast. Looking to flat-line your law firm? Looking to grow by 2% per-year? Please don't tune in. This is for law firm partners and marketing directors who are interested in exponential growth, not linear growth. Marketing has to do with more than just marketing. It has to do with how your firm is run, how good of a leader you are, and how you THINK. Think like a law firm growth wizard, with the Lawyer Marketing Lab Podcast.

title: Atlas IM Boards
author: ATLAS
description:
Internal medicine has more moving parts than anyone can hold in their head at once. Atlas IM Boards is here to help you carry them.
Each short episode takes one high-yield concept — the kind that shows up on the boards and in your clinic the same week — and explains it clearly enough to remember on the drive to work. No filler, no forty-minute detours. Just the core ideas, made memorable.
Whether you're revising for the exam or sharpening what you already use every day, these are the concepts worth keeping close.
From the physicians at Atlas Intensive Care.

title: Not Another Healer
author: Ki Marie
description:
For so long I've looked past myself for healing. For someone to tell me what's wrong and how to fix it - OH - And which way to go. I never looked within. Until now... My name is Ki ("Key"). I have been dreaming of starting a podcast for a long time... 
This is storytelling, connection, message, and reflection. We work together on a journey of healing from within. This is a work in progress! No music, no editing, no fabulous production. Just honest words for whomever needs to hear without the glamour and pizazz. Please, allow this process and growth as we embark on this new endeavor.

title: The Red Seal: A Mystery of Deception
description: In the heart of a bustling city, a series of puzzling events unfolds, drawing listeners into a web of intrigue. Natalie Sumner Lincoln invites you to explore the mysteries of 'Red Seal,' where nothing is as it appears. As you journey through this captivating detective tale, the line between truth and deception blurs with every twist and turn. Red seals signal danger, while red herrings lead you down unexpected paths, keeping you guessing until the very last moment. Packed with suspense and unexpected revelations, this audiobook immerses you in a world where every clue matters and every character has something to hide. Get ready for an enthralling experience that will challenge your wits and leave you craving resolution. Ideal for fans of classic detective fiction, 'Red Seal' promises to engage your mind and entertain your imagination.

title: The FPR Method Podcast
author: B.W. Sloane
description:
Real cases. Real patterns. Real investigations.
Learn how to identify suspects, uncover motive, reconstruct timelines, and recognize the hidden patterns behind violent crime using The FPR Method. Hosted by B.W. Sloane, this true crime podcast combines criminology and forensic astrology to teach forensic pattern recognition through real criminal cases. Whether you're fascinated by crime or dream of helping solve cases, each episode builds practical investigative skills you can apply. Continue your training with The FPR Method books, courses, and student community.

title: Juju & Gems
author: big mama juju
description: Welcome to JuJu & Gems with Big MaMa JuJu, where wisdom is shared, stories are told, and gems are dropped. This space is a portal into African Traditional Religions and the people who practice them. From social media trends and community conversations to my personal experiences, spiritual downloads, and interviews with fellow jujus, we'll dive into the topics that matter most to our community with honesty, humor, and a little bit of magic. Pull up a seat and let's talk juju.

title: Can We Say That Out Loud?
author: The Integrated Mom
description: The podcast where working moms tell the truth, hosted by Greer Van Dyck and Kara Fresk!

title: Optometry Profits Revealed with Peter Precht
author: Peter Precht
description:
Welcome to Optometry Profits Revealed with Peter Precth, your ultimate resource for achieving profitability in the eye care industry amidst modern disruptions. We delve into everything from online frame purchasing, innovative prescription programs, to state-of-the-art lead generation and sales software.

Join us as we help opticians, ophthalmologists, and optometrists navigate through these challenging times and grow their businesses to unprecedented heights. Learn from industry experts, successful eye care entrepreneurs, and our host's own wealth of experience in the field. Tune in and gain a clear vision of your path to eye care business success.

Get your free digital consultation here: https://eyecclearly.com/

title: Quiet Thoughts
author: Asiya Muhammad
description:
Welcome to my very first episode! In a world that moves so fast, I wanted to create a little corner of the internet where we can slow down. Today, I’m introducing myself and sharing the heart behind this podcast: a space dedicated to quiet reflections on faith, growth, and the ups and downs of girlhood. You don’t have to have it all figured out to be doing well. Thank you for listening and joining me on this journey.
#QuietThoughts #PersonalGrowth #DeenAndGirlhood #ReflectiveJournal #SlowGrowth #Asiya #Mindfulness

title: Starborn Parenting Podcast
author: Alexis
description: Rick Kubina, PhD, and Madeline Jürgensen, PhD, have spent their careers studying behavior. Rick has three children of his own and now two grandchildren. Madeline has two young children. In other words, they live the research at home and teach it for a living. Each week on the Starborn Parenting Podcast, they take decades of behavior research blended with their own personal experience and turn it into tools parents can use that same night. Expect honest stories from their own parenting and grandparenting, including some that ended in frustration and tears, plus straight answers about what drives your child's behavior and how to change it without bribes or threats. They skip the jargon, disagree with each other on air, and never claim to have parenting fully figured out. Instead, they approach family life the way true scientists do: always observing, always testing, and sharing what works so other parents can benefit. New episodes drop weekly.

title: The Whole Word Podcast
author: Dr. James Griffin
description:
The Whole Word Podcast is a teaching and discipleship podcast from WordVision Ministries, created to help believers read Scripture with confidence, clarity, and purpose. Each episode slows down the pace, opens the Bible with intentionality, and guides listeners through practical tools that make God’s Word feel accessible not overwhelming.
Rather than rushing through verses or offering surface‑level inspiration, The Whole Word Podcast focuses on understanding the whole story of Scripture: what God is saying, why it matters, and how it shapes the way we live today.

title: LastDaySoldiers
author: Josiah Burris
description:
This is a podcast for believers who are hungry for more than comfortable Christianity. It's a call to move beyond lukewarm faith and into a life of wholehearted devotion to Jesus Christ.
Through honest biblical teaching, personal reflection, and Spirit-led encouragement, each episode challenges listeners to grow in spiritual maturity, deepen their relationship with Christ, and walk in the fullness of their God-given identity.

title: The Bottom Line Up Front With Terry Wade Thompson
author: Terry Wade Thompson
description:
A podcast about Leadership, Lifestyle Advice, Mindset, Health and Fitness, Business, and much more . . 
My goal is to provide you with tips, tools, techniques, and resources that will empower you to:
🌟 Become the Leader you are meant to be
🧠 Become more mindful
💪 Establish better habits
🔥 Live your life more intentional every day
Discover more at:
Website: https://terrywadethompson.com/

X (Twitter): https://x.com/TerryWThompson
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/terrywadethompson/

title: Mind Fit For Football Podcast
author: Micheal
description:
Helping ambitious footballers and parents build the mindset, confidence and mental resilience needed to perform under pressure and reach the next level.
My job is simple: take the noise out of their heads so they play with more freedom.
Some episodes are just me, breaking down one idea you can use with your child this week. 
Others are honest conversations with coaching experts and ex-pros, as well.
If you have ever stood on the touchline thinking there is more in there, I do not know how to get it out of him. This is the show for you.

title: 57e
description: 57e - Beyond functionality, this digital space emphasizes creating a positive and secure environment for its users. Privacy protection and data security are treated as top priorities, giving users confidence. The platform also fosters a welcoming atmosphere by encouraging respectful communication and meaningful engagement. title: Point of Contact | A Würth Knowing Podcast
author: Würth Industry USA
description:
The Point of Contact podcast brings industry conversations closer to the people, ideas, and challenges shaping the future of supply chain, manufacturing, distribution, and beyond.
Powered by Würth Knowing, this podcast creates space for thoughtful conversations with experts, leaders, partners, and team members across the industry. Each episode explores the connections that move business forward, from customer relationships and operational insights to innovation, culture, and the real-world experiences behind the work.
Stick around, there's so much more Würth Knowing!

title: Beyond Her Veil
author: Maria Asare
description: Beyond Her Veil is a space where I share the things God is teaching me in real time. Through short reflections, Scripture, and honest conversations, I invite you into my journey of following Christ, growing in faith, and learning what it truly means to reveal, heal, and become who He created us to be. If you’re simply trying to know God more deeply, you’re in the right place.

title: Why Spiritual Sensitivity Is Important
author: Wisdom Akpan (Erick)
description:
In a world filled with noise, distractions, and endless voices competing for attention, spiritual sensitivity has never been more important. This podcast explores why every believer must develop the ability to discern God’s voice, recognize divine direction, and remain spiritually alert in these critical times.

“Spiritual Sensitivity Is Needed” dives deep into the importance of walking closely with God, growing in prayer, and strengthening your spiritual awareness. When you become spiritually sensitive, you begin to see beyond the physical, understand God’s leading, and avoid costly spiritual mistakes.

Join us as we discuss practical ways to sharpen your spiritual senses, deepen your relationship with God, and position yourself to hear clearly from the Holy Spirit. Whether you are seeking spiritual growth, clarity, or deeper intimacy with God, this message will inspire and challenge you.

Stay connected, stay prayerful, and stay spiritually alert.

title: Awake With God, Sleep in Peace
author: Esther
description:
Awake with God, Sleep in Peace
Welcome to Awake with God, Sleep in Peace, your daily place of prayer, hope, and encouragement.
Every sunrise is a fresh opportunity to walk with God, and every night is a chance to rest in His unfailing love. This podcast is dedicated to helping you begin each day with faith and end each evening with peace through powerful Christian prayers and the timeless truth of God's Word.
Join us every day for uplifting morning prayers, peaceful bedtime prayers, daily Bible devotionals, Scripture readings, declarations of faith, prayers for healing, protection, guidance, favour, family, finances, strength, and spiritual growth.
Whether you are facing uncertainty, seeking comfort, celebrating blessings, or simply longing for a closer relationship with God, each episode is created to encourage your heart, strengthen your faith, and remind you that you are never alone.
No matter where you are in the world, take a few moments each day to pause, pray, and experience God's presence.
New prayer episodes every day.
Follow Awake with God, Sleep in Peace and make prayer the first and last part of every day. Share this show with your family and friends so that more people can experience God's love, hope, and peace.
"In peace I will lie down and sleep, for You alone, Lord, make me dwell in safety." — Psalm 4:8
Awake with God, Sleep in Peace
Wake up with faith. Rest in God's peace. Live each day in His presence.

title: InspectaCAR Vehicle Inspection in Calgar
description:
InspectaCAR Vehicle Inspection in Calgary Discussing Car Inspection Calgary Standards


InspectaCAR presents an informative podcast dedicated to helping drivers understand the importance of vehicle inspection in Calgary and why a professional car inspection Calgary service plays a vital role in maintaining road safety, vehicle reliability, and long-term ownership value. This episode explores the complete inspection process while providing practical insights for first-time buyers, experienced motorists, fleet operators, and anyone interested in making informed automotive decisions.Throughout the discussion, listeners learn how comprehensive vehicle inspections evaluate critical systems that influence overall performance and safety. The conversation explains the importance of examining engines, transmissions, brakes, suspension components, steering systems, electrical equipment, lighting, tires, wheels, exhaust systems, cooling systems, batteries, and fluid levels. Every topic is presented in an easy-to-understand format that helps simplify technical concepts for everyday drivers.The podcast also examines how routine inspections help identify developing mechanical concerns before they become expensive repairs. Early detection of worn components, fluid leaks, brake wear, tire damage, suspension issues, and electrical faults allows owners to address problems proactively while reducing maintenance costs and improving overall vehicle dependability. Preventive maintenance remains one of the most effective strategies for extending vehicle lifespan and enhancing driving confidence.Another important segment focuses on pre-purchase inspections for used vehicles. Listeners discover why an independent inspection can provide valuable information regarding a vehicle's mechanical condition, structural integrity, accident history indicators, frame alignment, corrosion, previous repairs, and overall roadworthiness. Understanding these factors enables buyers to make confident purchasing decisions while avoiding unexpected repair expenses after ownership begins.Modern automotive technology continues transforming inspection procedures, and the episode explores how advanced diagnostic equipment supports accurate evaluations. Digital scanning systems, computerized diagnostics, electronic testing equipment, emission assessments, and precision measuring tools help technicians identify faults efficiently while improving inspection accuracy. These innovations contribute to reliable assessments and greater transparency throughout the evaluation process.Safety remains a central theme throughout the conversation. Properly functioning braking systems, steering components, suspension assemblies, lighting equipment, windshield visibility, tire condition, and safety restraints all contribute to protecting drivers, passengers, and other road users. The discussion explains why regular inspections help maintain compliance with recommended safety standards while promoting responsible vehicle ownership.The episode further highlights the benefits of maintaining complete service records and inspection documentation. Organized maintenance histories support resale value, improve buyer confidence, simplify future servicing, and demonstrate responsible ownership. Listeners also receive practical advice regarding seasonal maintenance, winter driving preparation, tire condition monitoring, battery performance, fluid replacement schedules, and general vehicle care throughout changing weather conditions.Whether preparing for a road trip, purchasing a pre-owned vehicle, maintaining a family automobile, or managing a commercial fleet, this podcast delivers educational content designed to improve automotive knowledge and encourage informed maintenance decisions. The discussion combines professional insights with practical recommendations that drivers can easily understand and apply.Designed for motorists of every experience level, this episode serves as a valuable educational resource covering inspection procedures, preventive maintenance, vehicle safety, diagnostic technology, mechanical reliability, and responsible ownership practices. Through engaging conversation and industry-focused information, listeners gain a deeper appreciation for the value of professional vehicle inspection in Calgary and dependable car inspection Calgary services while learning how routine inspections contribute to safer driving, improved performance, greater reliability, and long-term vehicle protection.


To Know More Visit : https://inspectacar.ca/

title: Inclusion Starts Here: The EYSEN Podcast

description: Inclusion Starts Here, presented by Best Practice Network, is a podcast dedicated to exploring the people, ideas and actions that make inclusion a reality. Find out more at https://www.bestpracticenet.co.uk/early-years-SENCO

title: The Gathering Hub Podcast
author: The Gathering Hub Podcast
description: The Gathering Hub Podcast Hosted by Enrique "El Kike" Moreno, Juan "Whitey" Hernandez, and Maximiliano "B-wop" Lara is a place to gather and have discussions from funny to topical subjects, and anything in between. With Possibilities of special appearances from friends to gather their opinion on said subjects.

title: Breaking Bread powered by Harmonic
author: Bread and Jam
description: Building a food or drink brand is exciting, but it’s also full of expensive mistakes waiting to happen. Breaking Bread is the podcast that helps you avoid them. Hosted by Jason Gibb, co-founder of Bread & Jam, each episode gets real with founders, investors, buyers, and industry pros who have been through it all - the wins, the lows, and everything in between. Whether you are just getting started or scaling at speed, this is your go-to podcast for real talk, practical advice, and a community that believes success tastes better when shared.

title: Story World
author: story world
description:
Welcome to Story World — where every story comes alive.

From thrilling adventures and chilling mysteries to heartfelt tales and unforgettable life experiences, there’s always a new story waiting for you. Sit back, relax, and let your imagination travel to worlds beyond your own.

New stories uploaded regularly. Follow and join the journey—because every story deserves to be heard.

title: Human Scale: Technology, Attention, and the Art of Staying Human
author: William Carty
description: My name is William Carty. I’m a dad, a writer, and I spent close to thirty years working in IT, helping wire up the same digital systems I find myself wondering about these days. Human Scale is my attempt to pay attention to how technology, community, and daily life fit together. Mostly, I’m interested in what it takes to stay human in a world that keeps speeding up.

title: Echo chamber
author: WESTINO
description:
Storytellers, Creators & Change-Makers Across Every Industry
Note From The Host: My ideal guest is anyone with a compelling story, hard-won wisdom, or a unique perspective that connects to music, creativity, purpose, or the human experience. Whether you're an artist, entrepreneur, author, coach, athlete, or industry professional. if your story resonates, Echo Chamber wants to hear it.

title: A Different Game
author: Golfran Rivera
description:
Most people work hard their whole lives and still feel stuck. Not because they're lazy, but because nobody taught them the rules of the game.
A Different Game is a story-driven series for young adults who are ready to start thinking differently about purpose, money, and building a life on their own terms. Each season follows a new character navigating real challenges and learning the mental frameworks that actually change outcomes.
This isn't self-help. It's a story. But it might change the way you think.
New seasons dropping regularly. Start with Episode 1.

title: The After Church Podcast with Mike and Tabitha Harder
author: ALIGHT
description: We’re Pastor Mike and Tabitha Harder, and we hope our discussions are a blessing to you as we navigate relationships, parenting, ministry, culture, and living alight with authentic faith in Jesus.

title: Let Us Cook
author: Chef Kevin
description:
Who has time to learn a new hobby? My guests certainly don't. They're too busy being successful/famous/cool.
On Let Us Cook, I spend one month learning something they've always wanted to try, but haven't. My hope is to convince them to finally give it a go.

title: Black Creek Baptist Church
author: Black Creek Baptist Church
description:
King James Bible Preaching services from Black Creek Baptist Church in Black Creek New York.

A variety of topics.

Visit our ministry website at Onesoulatatime.net

title: Dead Ink
author: Dead Ink
description:
Every tattoo tells a story. Most of them are darker than you'd expect.


Dead Ink is a weekly anthology podcast about the hidden history of tattooing — the codes, the criminals, the outcasts, and the moments when ink changed everything. Russian prison gangs. Yakuza masters. Circus performers. Naval superstitions. Forensic detectives. Each episode takes one story from the underground history of marked skin and tells it the way it deserves to be told: with precision, without sensationalism, and with everything that makes it strange.

New episodes every week.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Unlocking The Paradigm Portal
author: Sarah C.
description:
You already know something needs to change. You've known for a while.
Unlocking the Paradigm Portal is the invitation to stop performing okay and actually look. Into the attachment wounds. Into the nervous system. Into the stories you forgot were stories.
Hosted by Sarah — attachment coach, somatic practitioner, someone who has sat in her own wreckage and decided to look anyway.
Hard-hitting mornings. Soft nights. Deep Sundays.
This is not self-help. This is self-honesty.

title: The Rx Files
author: The Vanguard Network
description:
The pharmaceutical industry touches every one of us, but most of us don't understand how it works — or why. Why does it cost so much to bring a new drug to market? Who's actually setting the prices you pay at the pharmacy? Is Big Pharma hiding the cure for cancer? And why does the US lead the world in drug discovery, yet rank among the worst in affordability?

The Rx Files is here to answer those questions — in plain English, without the jargon and without the spin.

Hosted by Nancy Lurker, a 40-year pharmaceutical industry veteran who has worked across Big Pharma, the service sector, and biotech - including as a CEO - alongside veteran journalist Ken Stone, The Rx Files brings frank, informed, and often provocative conversation to one of the most consequential industries on earth. Nancy knows the industry's strengths and its warts, and she's not afraid to talk about both. Ken is there to ask the questions the rest of us would ask if we had the chance.

Each episode, they'll go deep on a different piece of the puzzle — drug discovery, clinical trials, pricing, regulation, the role of insurance and pharmacy benefit managers, and much more. At every step they’ll be joined by guests who are actually in the room where it happens.

And of course they’ll be tackling all the questions you’d like to hear answered! You can reach the intrepid team at answers@TheRXFiles.com or comment directly at the end of the episodes.

The Rx Files is produced by The Vanguard Network and made possible with the support of Syneos Health.

title: Don't Make It Worse
author: Makenzie Mizell
description:
Everything is political, but that doesn’t mean everything has to be a fight.
Each week Makenzie Mizell talks to experts and people with lived experience - sometimes it’s serious and sometimes it’s fun. From sex work to prison abolition, we unpack the subjects people usually argue about through curiosity, compassion, and a willingness to ask questions.
We’re looking to make things better. You’re invited.

title: Bible Stories
author: True Crime Stories
description: Welcome to my channel, where we will delve into the rich and inspiring narratives of biblical stories.

title: Nightmail
author: Nightmail
description: Stop at the station from time to time because you never know who is driving the train or what new entertainment it will the deliver. If you like to laugh, enjoy unique conversations and thirst for a new stream of entertainment, this is your stop.

title: Mostly Domestic: A Generational Podcast
author: Joy and Ley
description: Two women, two generations, and countless opinions. Join Joy and Ley as they dive into relationships, family, pop culture, and the everyday moments that connect us. From hilarious stories to honest conversations, Mostly Domestic is where different generations come together to laugh, learn, and keep it real.

title: The Best News Ever
author: The Best News Ever Team
description: The Best News Ever is a podcast about one message that changes everything: Jesus died for your sins, was buried, and rose again. That finished work reconciles you back to God, and it settles your hope, your peace, and your assurance in who you are. Here we teach the gospel of grace without the religious fog, so you understand what Christ already accomplished, who you are in Christ because of it, and how to walk out that identity in everyday life. This is a place to dive into Scripture, work through Bible studies, and find resources that help you understand God's Word and stand on what grace has already made true about you.

title: Avoid Scams
author: Orientation to Ontario (O2O)
description: This O2O podcast explains common scams in Ontario, including fake websites, emails, texts, and phone calls. It highlights warning signs, such as requests for personal information or money, and offers tips to stay safe, emphasizing that government agencies will not make threatening demands or ask for payments this way. [orientatio...ontario.ca]

title: Drinking with Dawn
author: Dawn Mushill
description:
Welcome to Drinking with Dawn, hosted by Dawn Mushill, President & CEO of the Troy/Maryville/St. Jacob/Marine Chamber of Commerce. 
Each episode features authentic conversations with local business leaders, entrepreneurs, professionals, and community changemakers who are making a positive impact in our communities. 
Grab your favorite drink and join us for conversations that inspire connection, leadership, and growth.

title: Between Chapters
author: Aminie and Diya Nuri
description:
Welcome to Between Chapters!
The podcast where books and real life collide. As two friends who love getting lost in a good story, we're diving into the chapters that shape us— our latest reads, fandom obsessions, life updates, lessons learned, and the chapters we're currently writing. Think of it as a cozy conversation between friends, where books meet real life and every chapter matters. Join us as we discuss the moments that turn the page on who we are becoming.

title: The Muse Talk Podcast
author: Harrychrisrobin
description:
The Muse Talk Podcast is the home for Muse fans. Hosted by Harry and Josh, we explore every aspect of the band's incredible career—from Matt Bellamy's legendary guitar tones and gear to album deep-dives, song analysis, live performances, and the stories behind the music.


Alongside retrospective discussions, we cover the latest Muse news, releases, tours, and events, bringing fans together to celebrate one of modern rock's most innovative bands.


Whether you've followed Muse since Showbiz or you're just discovering their music, The Muse Talk Podcast delivers engaging conversations, expert insights, and a passionate fan community dedicated to all things Muse.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Book Stuff
author: Tucker Legerski
description:
Book Stuff is a podcast that explores how literature helps us live our lives and navigate our world. For writers and readers, and those who are reading curious, we dive into it all. From forgotten fiction to buzzy sci-fi, poetry readings to libraries, publishers, clubs, Substacks to journalism – all texts and literary worlds are considered. 
In a so-called post-literate world, we celebrate, advocate for, and talk about the still-existing power — and fun — of the written word in literature and beyond.

And while I’ve got your attention, you may come across some obviously fake, AI-generated videos using my face and name. There seems to be a new channel every week, something that’s becoming increasingly common among YouTube doctors unfortunately...

Please be assured that I am not behind any of these and do not endorse any of the information coming from these quite frightening AI versions of me.

Let’s spread the word and help prevent any dangerous medical misinformation being shared like this.

Myia examines every case with detailed research, verified sources, psychological profiling, and a commitment to accuracy. Listeners get clear timelines, offender behavior analysis, victim-centered narratives, and ongoing follow-ups as cases evolve.

This isn’t just a narrated crime show—it’s a full audio experience. Custom sound design, original FX, and atmospheric sound beds create a cinematic feeling that places you inside the investigation while keeping the focus on facts, context, and truth.

Among Monsters is built for true crime listeners who want depth, clarity, journalism, and storytelling that feels alive. If you follow serial killers, unsolved murders, missing persons investigations, or high-detail case analysis, this podcast is your new essential listen.

Where investigative reporting meets cinematic true crime.

author: Trevor
description: The show outlines a radical soccer strategy called the Pragmatic System, which prioritizes winning over aesthetic appeal. The argument is that teams should reject the "beautiful game" and instead, for example, use long balls to bypass risky passing in their own defensive zones.
